message:
perf(string): single-pass strstr absent scan with NUL termination (bd-dom7k)

The previous strstr implementation did a full strlen on the haystack
up front, then walked windows() across the full slice. For the absent
case (the dominant workload — scanning until a sentinel rules out a
match) that is two passes: one to compute h_len and one for the
windows scan. On a haystack of N safely-padded bytes ending in NUL
we touched roughly 2N bytes even when the first byte already ruled
out the needle.

This commit replaces the two-pass form with a single-pass terminator-
aware streaming search that:

  * scans the haystack byte-by-byte from index 0;
  * short-circuits and returns None the moment a NUL byte is seen
    (matching glibc strstr's semantics — strstr stops at the C string
    terminator, not at slice length);
  * skips the inner-loop comparison entirely on bytes that don't
    match needle[0] (the cheap pre-filter that strstr's FastSearch
    family also exploits);
  * confirms candidate matches with a needle-length inner walk that
    re-checks for NUL on every haystack byte so a partially-matched
    candidate that runs into the terminator correctly returns None.

The empty-needle (n_len == 0) early-return is preserved unchanged.

Three new regression tests pin the contract:

  * test_strstr_stops_at_terminator — haystack "a\0bc\0", needle
    "bc\0" must return None even though "bc" appears past the NUL.
    This is the regression that motivated the rewrite: the old
    windows()-over-strlen-prefix happened to get this right, but the
    new code's NUL-aware short-circuit needs explicit coverage.
  * test_strstr_unterminated_haystack_match — haystack "abc"
    (no NUL), needle "bc\0", returns Some(1). Confirms the loop
    terminates cleanly when running off the end of an unterminated
    slice without a NUL pre-stop.
  * test_strstr_unterminated_haystack_short_candidate — haystack
    "ab", needle "bc\0", returns None. Pins the i + n_len > len
    boundary case where the candidate would run past the slice.

A new Criterion bench bench_strstr_absent (sizes 16, 64, 256, 1024,
4096) is added to the string_bench harness and registered in the
criterion_group target list so before/after regression evidence
stays inside the existing bench surface.

The .beads/issues.jsonl entry for bd-dom7k is opened to in_progress
in this commit; closure follows once Criterion before/after numbers
and the full conformance/UBS/clippy gate land cleanly under rch.

 {"id":"bd-dg3r","title":"syscall: io_uring sqpoll veneer parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:50.911253411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:11:56.041095355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:11:56.038531722Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-di5w","title":"Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry","description":"## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic — streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:17.216902967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935547570Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935252511Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io-internal","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1361,"issue_id":"bd-di5w","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dlf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc dlfcn.h diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 5 functions: dlopen+dlsym+dlclose round-trip on libm.so.6 with cos symbol (compares both null-status and exact address since shared lib resolves to same address), dlopen nonexistent NULL, dlsym RTLD_DEFAULT for getpid, dlsym unknown symbol NULL, dladdr resolves libc::malloc to a known module (compares dli_fbase). 6 tests, 0 divergences against glibc. dlclose with bogus handle skipped (UB, glibc rtld segfaults).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:45:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","dlfcn"]}
+{"id":"bd-dom7k","title":"Optimize core strstr absent scan with benchmark proof","description":"Spec-first parity/performance slice for safe Rust byte substring search: benchmark strstr absent behavior, replace the haystack strlen pre-scan plus windows scan with a terminator-aware streaming search only if Criterion shows a worthwhile opportunity, and verify core string tests, ABI string tests, glibc conformance diff, fmt/check/clippy, UBS, and before/after benchmark evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:52:39.512191974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:53:15.963509802Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","performance","string"]}
 {"id":"bd-dov2m","title":"PORT: __strchrnul / __memrchr / __getline (glibc reserved-namespace aliases)","description":"Port the glibc reserved-namespace aliases for three already-shipped libc primitives: __strchrnul, __memrchr, and __getline. glibc exports both the public name (strchrnul, memrchr, getline) and the leading-underscore namespaced name; some headers and a few third-party callers (notably glibcs own internal headers and certain RH toolchain shims) link against the underscored variant. Implementation: thin abi shims that delegate to the existing public-name implementations. Tests: each alias produces identical output to its public counterpart on a representative input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:11:40.411351669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:16:25.121117240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:16:25.119090831Z","close_reason":"shipped: __strchrnul / __memrchr / __getline reserved-namespace aliases delegating to existing public-name impls + 8 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dq9t","title":"setjmp: execute setjmp_nested_edges fixture programs through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/setjmp_nested_edges_conformance_test.rs only validates tests/conformance/fixtures/setjmp_nested_edges.json metadata. Add executable coverage for the documented program scenarios through the harness matrix or its deterministic integration path, assert strict and hardened expectations for nested longjmp and sigmask round-trip behavior, and preserve explicit unsupported-scenario documentation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:06:43.306090252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:42.520337201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:42.518294051Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic execution coverage for setjmp nested-edge fixture programs across strict and hardened profiles.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dqbv","title":"nss: execute grp_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/grp_ops_conformance_test.rs currently validates fixture metadata and coverage counts only. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/grp_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:43:33.470596927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.475094946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.472922473Z","close_reason":"Added isolated harness-matrix execution for grp_ops.json and verified locally and via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
